message:
br-cgodw: fuzz_dlfcn harness respects State::Live for dlsym/dlclose

Two crashes from one root cause: the harness called dlsym and dlclose
on handles that had already been dlclose'd to refcount=0, which is UB
per POSIX. glibc's dlclose on invalid handle corrupts loader state,
which later manifests as a SEGV inside ld-linux during dlsym on an
unrelated LIVE handle — a cross-operation state corruption.

Fix: Op::Sym, Op::SymFuzz, Op::Addr, and Op::Close all early-return
when h.state != State::Live. Close is the key one; without it, repeated
Close on a stale slot continues to poison the loader.

Preserved both reproducers as corpus regression entries:
  crash-81b48dad… (direct dlsym-on-stale)
  crash-55a8bcc1… (SymFuzz after triple-Close on a different slot)

177K runs clean after fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-cdqp","title":"ssignal_and_gsignal_deliver_signal test fails (SIGNAL_HIT is 0)","description":"The `ssignal_and_gsignal_deliver_signal` test in `unistd_abi_test.rs` fails because the signal handler installed by `ssignal` is not observing `SIGUSR1` when raised via `gsignal`. The `gsignal` call returns 0 (success), but `SIGNAL_HIT.load()` remains 0 instead of 10. This indicates a bug in the signal trampoline, `sysv_signal`'s translation, or signal delivery latency/blocking.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:24:26.488095325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:29:47.257390429Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:29:47.255345646Z","close_reason":"Current tree already fixed: ssignal/gsignal delivery and errno parity tests pass via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cfm1","title":"Printf DISC-001: Implement banker's rounding (round-half-to-even)","description":"Implement IEEE 754 round-half-to-even (banker's rounding) for %.0f and similar precision-limited float formatting. Currently uses round-half-away-from-zero which diverges from glibc.

Reference: sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) should return "2" not "3".
Spec: C11 7.21.6.1 footnote says rounding unspecified but POSIX requires IEEE 754 conformance which specifies round-half-to-even.","acceptance_criteria":"- sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 1.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 3.5) returns "4"
- All printf conformance tests pass including round_half_even
- DISC-001 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:00:00Z","created_by":"claude","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:18:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-14T14:18:00Z","close_reason":"FIXED: Changed format_f and format_e in printf.rs to use round_ties_even() instead of round(). Added test_format_float_bankers_rounding test. Updated DISCREPANCIES.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","discrepancy","ieee754","printf"]}
 {"id":"bd-cfm2","title":"Scanf DISC-004: Implement hex float parsing (0x1.fp+2)","description":"Implement C11 hex float format parsing for scanf %a/%A specifiers. Currently hex floats like 0x1.fp+2 (= 7.75) fail to parse.

Format: 0x[h...h][.h...h][pN] where h is hex digit, N is decimal exponent.
Spec: C11 7.21.6.2 paragraph 12 describes %a/%A hex float format.","acceptance_criteria":"- sscanf("0x1.fp+2", "%a", &f) parses correctly to 7.75
- sscanf("0x1.0p10", "%a", &f) parses to 1024.0
- sscanf("0x1p0", "%a", &f) parses to 1.0
- All scanf hex float conformance tests pass
- DISC-004 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:00:01Z","created_by":"claude","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:20:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-14T14:20:00Z","close_reason":"FIXED: Implemented scan_hex_float in scanf.rs to parse hex floats. Added 5 unit tests covering basic, integer, negative, negative exponent, and uppercase cases. Updated DISCREPANCIES.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","discrepancy","ieee754","scanf"]}
+{"id":"bd-cgodw","title":"fuzz_dlfcn harness: dlsym/dladdr on stale dlclose'd handle passes UB pointer to ld-linux","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_dlfcn.rs Op::Sym/Op::SymFuzz/Op::Addr call dlsym on handles returned from pick_slot without checking State::Live. When a prior Close op transitioned the handle to Stale, subsequent Sym ops pass a freed handle into real libc dlsym, which dereferences it inside the dynamic loader and SEGV's. Reproducer: artifacts/fuzz_dlfcn/crash-81b48dad2c4463a9ba111dda2a7debed80d78913. Same shape as bd-2t616 (pidfd_timer), bd-reb0e (TimerDelete), earlier fuzz_socket/mmap stale fixes. Fix: skip op when h.state != State::Live. Harness bug, not a library bug.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:09:59.917913944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:17:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:17:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Fixed: Sym/SymFuzz/Addr and Close now skip stale handles. dlclose on already-closed handle corrupts glibc loader state; manifests as later-SEGV on dlsym of unrelated LIVE handle. 177K runs clean after fix."}
 {"id":"bd-cgqe","title":"pattern: execute regex_glob_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/regex_glob_ops_conformance_test.rs still executes tests/conformance/fixtures/regex_glob_ops.json through the in-process TestRunner path. Replace that coverage with isolated harness subprocess execution, assert expected_output plus host parity for regcomp/regexec/fnmatch/glob/wordexp in strict and hardened modes, and fix any matrix-path mismatches the new executable coverage exposes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:06:43.126457693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:09:31.175231942Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:09:31.173166789Z","close_reason":"Replaced in-process regex_glob_ops TestRunner coverage with isolated harness subprocess execution and verified locally plus via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cipv","title":"signal: execute signal_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/signal_ops_conformance_test.rs currently exercises signal fixtures only through frankenlibc_fixture_exec. Add isolated harness subprocess coverage for tests/conformance/fixtures/signal_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity by mode, and fix any subprocess-path signal parity regressions the matrix run exposes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:24:52.703709183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:38:41.553830925Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T12:38:41.550944201Z","close_reason":"Switched signal_ops fixture coverage to the isolated harness conformance matrix subprocess path and verified locally plus via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cj0","title":"Syscall core: introduce raw Linux x86_64 syscall veneer (no libc::syscall)","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Deliverables:
- Add a minimal raw syscall veneer module with typed wrappers for read/write/openat/close/mmap/munmap/mprotect/futex/clone/exit.
- No libc::syscall usage in replacement profile paths.
- Invariants doc: argument marshalling, errno handling, restart semantics.

Acceptance:
- Symbol-level tests confirm wrappers return glibc-compatible errno semantics.
- rg check proves replacement profile call graph contains zero libc::syscall invocations.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.109281820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:47.599408Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:47.599408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","p0-path","syscall"]}

These exercise: socket operations, SSL/TLS (via OpenSSL), readline, locale-aware formatting, signal handling.

Fix any crashes, then test with actual database connections.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:51.900137264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:52.118219604Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:50.809758463Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.1","depends_on_id":"bd-s39o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:51.900137264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1337,"issue_id":"bd-s39o.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"sqlite3, psql, mysql not installed on this system. Cannot test. Deferred.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-s39o.2","title":"Test and fix build tools under LD_PRELOAD (cmake, ninja, gcc)","description":"Test cmake, ninja, and gcc under LD_PRELOAD.
These exercise: fork/exec heavily, pipe/waitpid, tempfile creation, stat/lstat trees, environment manipulation.
Start with --version, then test actual build operations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:53.059194748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:29.300141372Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:28.041737230Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.2","depends_on_id":"bd-s39o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:53.059194748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1336,"issue_id":"bd-s39o.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"gcc/g++ --version now works. Actual compilation via gcc has a -plugin linker argument issue (ld gets wrong args). Tracked separately.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-s39o.3","title":"Test and fix network diagnostic tools under LD_PRELOAD (wget, dig, nslookup)","description":"Test wget, dig, nslookup, host under LD_PRELOAD.
These exercise: DNS resolution (getaddrinfo beyond /etc/hosts), socket connect, SSL/TLS, HTTP parsing.
Our getaddrinfo only supports numeric hosts and /etc/hosts lookups. For DNS resolution, we'd need to either:
1. Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric hosts
2. Implement a DNS resolver stub
Option 1 is more practical for now.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31.400999331Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:30.152849132Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-cmiv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:16.988805266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-s39o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1338,"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"wget resolves DNS, connects, but GnuTLS has thread locking error (separate SSL issue). curl works perfectly including HTTPS. dig/nslookup are statically linked (can't preload). host works.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-s83dv","title":"fuzz_pathname harness: leaks realpath(NULL) return instead of freeing","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pathname.rs:153-158 calls realpath(cs.as_ptr(), NULL), gets a malloc'd buffer, and deliberately leaks it (comment: 'leak in the fuzz target is acceptable'). LeakSanitizer flags this and exits with 77 as soon as a path bytes combination makes realpath succeed (resolving to something short, e.g. 2 bytes). Effect: short fuzz runs fail on an infrastructural leak, masking any real library-side leak. Fix: unsafe { libc::free(ret as *mut c_void) } after archetype completes. Our realpath at stdlib_abi.rs:4050 uses libc::malloc so libc::free is the right pair. Reproducer: crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/artifacts/fuzz_pathname/leak-f413061db6eee949e03cba98bbb1b4526ec3525c","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:01:39.200746584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:01:39.200746584Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-s83dv","title":"fuzz_pathname harness: leaks realpath(NULL) return instead of freeing","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pathname.rs:153-158 calls realpath(cs.as_ptr(), NULL), gets a malloc'd buffer, and deliberately leaks it (comment: 'leak in the fuzz target is acceptable'). LeakSanitizer flags this and exits with 77 as soon as a path bytes combination makes realpath succeed (resolving to something short, e.g. 2 bytes). Effect: short fuzz runs fail on an infrastructural leak, masking any real library-side leak. Fix: unsafe { libc::free(ret as *mut c_void) } after archetype completes. Our realpath at stdlib_abi.rs:4050 uses libc::malloc so libc::free is the right pair. Reproducer: crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/artifacts/fuzz_pathname/leak-f413061db6eee949e03cba98bbb1b4526ec3525c","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:01:39.200746584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:06:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:06:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: free realpath/canonicalize_file_name returns with libc::free in fuzz_pathname harness. Landed in 9e170061. 5.4M runs clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-sbt21","title":"sprintf executor returns 'unsupported printf arg value' for float %e","description":"execute_sprintf_case in frankenlibc_conformance cannot dispatch certain printf conversions (observed: sprintf_e_basic with 12345.0).  {"id":"bd-sdov","title":"Conformance: direct execute process_ops fixture cases","description":"Add direct execute_fixture_case coverage for process_ops.json and extend the shared executor for safe process fixtures such as getpid/getppid/fork/waitpid, correcting fixture expectations for POSIX wait semantics if needed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T11:40:07.819647246Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T11:52:26.960881901Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T11:52:26.958979169Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for process_ops fixtures, implemented safe process executor coverage for getpid/getppid/fork/waitpid, and corrected WNOHANG/no-child expectation to ECHILD; verified with rch focused test/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","process","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-sh2ms","title":"syscall: pidfd_getfd self pidfd with invalid flags should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for pidfd_getfd(self_pidfd, 1, 1). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the invalid-flags contract on a valid pidfd.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:52:52.477053379Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:54:24.500827639Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:54:24.498299060Z","close_reason":"pidfd_getfd(self, 0, 1) → EINVAL (non-zero flags reserved) pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
